Title: Induction of γ‐aminobutyric acid plays a positive role to Arabidopsis resistance against Pseudomonas syringae

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Positive role in plant immunity against bacterial pathogen; associated with PTI and ETI resistance
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLP-dependent glutamate decarboxylase; Ca2+/calmodulin-responsive C-terminal autoinhibitory/CaM-binding domain
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for pathogen-induced GABA accumulation, together with GAD4
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Mainly expressed in leaves and roots at vegetative stage; inducible by MPK3/MPK6 activation and by Pst/Pst-avrRpt2, especially avrRpt2-triggered immunity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in leaves and roots at vegetative stage; strongly induced after DEX activation of MPK3/MPK6 and after Pst-avrRpt2 inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen-induced GABA accumulation and contributes positively to resistance against Pst and Pst-avrRpt2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout crossed into DD background; RT-qPCR; GABA quantification; higher-order mutant analysis; pathogen infection assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DEX-induced MPK3/MPK6 activation; inoculation with Pst, Pst-avrRpt2, or Pst-hrcC-
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to optimize disease resistance through moderate enhancement of GABA biosynthesis; should be balanced with other GAD genes to avoid excessive GABA accumulation-associated penalties
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overaccumulation of GABA in truncated GAD overexpression lines caused dwarf/yellow phenotype and reduced resistance; balanced GABA level is critical
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Loss of gad1 alone reduced MPK3/MPK6-induced GABA accumulation by ~35% in DD plants, but single gad1 mutant had little effect on GABA levels after Pst-avrRpt2 due to redundancy with GAD2/GAD4
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAD1 is strongly induced by MPK3/MPK6 activation and by Pst-avrRpt2 infection. 2. GAD1 contributes to MPK3/MPK6-mediated GABA biosynthesis and works with GAD2 and GAD4 to sustain pathogen-induced GABA accumulation. 3. Reduced GABA biosynthesis in higher-order gad mutants compromises resistance to both Pst and Pst-avrRpt2.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression profiling data in GVG-NtMEK2DD plants (accession NA) used for candidate identification
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GAD1 promoter:GUS (reporter); CaMV 35S promoter used only for truncated overexpression constructs in related GAD studies within this paper

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Positive role in plant immunity when maintained at appropriate levels; supports basal and induced GABA pool
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLP-dependent glutamate decarboxylase; Ca2+/calmodulin-responsive C-terminal autoinhibitory/CaM-binding domain
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major contributor to basal and pathogen-induced GABA levels in shoots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most abundant GAD transcript in leaves; constitutively expressed in leaves and roots; downregulated after MPK3/MPK6 activation and pathogen inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in leaves and roots at vegetative stage; downregulated after DEX-induced MPK3/MPK6 activation and after Pst/Pst-avrRpt2 inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Maintains basal and pathogen-induced GABA levels; contributes positively to resistance through sustaining GABA biosynthesis, but not the main induced isoform under MPK3/MPK6 activation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; RT-qPCR; GABA quantification in single and higher-order mutants; pathogen infection assay; truncated overexpression (GAD2ΔC)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DEX-induced MPK3/MPK6 activation; inoculation with Pst, Pst-avrRpt2, or Pst-hrcC-
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be leveraged to elevate basal GABA-associated defense capacity, but constitutive hyperactivation is undesirable due to severe growth defects
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutively active truncated GAD2ΔC causes massive GABA overaccumulation, yellow/dwarf phenotype, and reduced resistance due to likely metabolic imbalance and pathogen nutritional benefit
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mutation of gad2 reduced baseline GABA strongly but had no significant effect on MPK3/MPK6-induced GABA burst in DD plants; nevertheless, gad2 single mutant significantly lowered both basal and pathogen-induced GABA in shoots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAD2 is the predominant contributor to basal GABA in Arabidopsis leaves and also supports pathogen-induced GABA accumulation. 2. Its transcript is downregulated by the MKK4/MKK5-MPK3/MPK6-WRKY33 pathway during Pst-avrRpt2 response. 3. Hyperactive GAD2ΔC causes excessive GABA accumulation, growth defects, and reduced disease resistance, indicating that balanced GABA level is essential.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GAD2 promoter:GUS (reporter); CaMV 35S promoter for Flag-GAD2ΔC overexpression

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Positive role in plant immunity through inducible GABA biosynthesis during pathogen response
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PLP-dependent glutamate decarboxylase; Ca2+/calmodulin-responsive C-terminal autoinhibitory/CaM-binding domain
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: major inducible control step under MPK3/MPK6 activation and avrRpt2-triggered immunity
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low basal expression; mainly expressed in leaves and roots at vegetative stage; extremely strongly induced by MPK3/MPK6 activation and strongly induced by Pst-avrRpt2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in leaves and roots at vegetative stage; ~23,000-fold induced by DEX activation of MPK3/MPK6 and ~100-fold induced by Pst-avrRpt2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly promotes inducible GABA accumulation during MPK3/MPK6 activation and contributes to disease resistance as part of GAD1/2/4 module
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout crossed into DD background; RT-qPCR; GABA quantification; higher-order mutant analysis; pathogen infection assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DEX-induced MPK3/MPK6 activation; inoculation with Pst, Pst-avrRpt2, or Pst-hrcC-
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful inducible target for boosting defense-associated GABA biosynthesis if expression is tuned; may be preferable to constitutive hyperactivation strategies
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessive activation of GAD activity can cause GABA overaccumulation-related growth penalties as shown with truncated GAD overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Loss of gad4 caused ~85% reduction of MPK3/MPK6-induced GABA in DD plants, showing especially strong contribution to MAPK-driven inducible GABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GAD4 is one of the most highly induced genes after MPK3/MPK6 activation. 2. GAD4 is a major driver of inducible GABA biosynthesis during avrRpt2-triggered immunity. 3. Its induction depends on the MKK4/MKK5-MPK3/MPK6-WRKY33 signaling cascade.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression profiling data in GVG-NtMEK2DD plants (accession NA) used for candidate identification
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native GAD4 promoter:GUS (reporter); CaMV 35S promoter used only for truncated overexpression constructs in related GAD studies within this paper

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GAD1 and GAD4 induction; contributes to suppression of GAD2 during immune response
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: MAPK signaling leading to transcriptional regulation via downstream TF WRKY33
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pst infection; Pst-avrRpt2-triggered immunity; upstream MKK4/MKK5; DEX activation in GVG-NtMEK2DD system
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen-responsive GAD1/GAD4 expression and GABA accumulation; supports defense against Pst-avrRpt2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major immune-responsive controller of inducible GABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic loss-of-function in mpk3 and DD mpk3 backgrounds; RT-qPCR; GABA quantification after DEX or pathogen treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activated rapidly in plant immunity; experimentally activated by DEX in DD system
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream target to coordinate defense metabolite induction, though pleiotropy likely due to broad immune roles
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad defense regulator with likely pleiotropic effects beyond GABA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Loss of MPK3 reduced GAD1/GAD4 induction and compromised MPK3/MPK6-driven GABA accumulation; however total GABA after Pst-avrRpt2 was not reduced in mpk3 single mutant, likely due to compensation including unsuppressed GAD2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MPK3 activation strongly promotes GAD1/GAD4 expression and GABA accumulation. 2. mpk3 mutation suppresses pathogen-induced GAD1/GAD4 transcription. 3. MPK3 functions redundantly with MPK6 upstream of WRKY33 in regulating inducible GABA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression profiling in GVG-NtMEK2DD system (accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GVG-inducible NtMEK2DD system upstream of MPK3/MPK6 activation

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates GAD1 and GAD4 induction; contributes to suppression of GAD2 during immune response
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: MAPK signaling leading to transcriptional regulation via downstream TF WRKY33
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Pst infection; Pst-avrRpt2-triggered immunity; upstream MKK4/MKK5; DEX activation in GVG-NtMEK2DD system
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen-responsive GAD1/GAD4 expression and GABA accumulation; supports defense against Pst-avrRpt2
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major immune-responsive controller of inducible GABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic loss-of-function in mpk6 and DD mpk6 backgrounds; RT-qPCR; GABA quantification after DEX or pathogen treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activated rapidly in plant immunity; experimentally activated by DEX in DD system
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream target to coordinate defense metabolite induction, though with strong pleiotropic immune consequences
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad immune regulator with pleiotropic effects beyond GABA metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Loss of MPK6 reduced GAD1/GAD4 induction and partially compromised MPK3/MPK6-driven GABA accumulation; redundancy with MPK3 evident
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MPK6 redundantly functions with MPK3 to induce GAD1/GAD4 expression. 2. mpk6 mutation suppresses pathogen-induced GAD1/GAD4 transcription. 3. MPK6 is part of the signaling module controlling inducible GABA biosynthesis during immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Expression profiling in GVG-NtMEK2DD system (accession NA)
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: GVG-inducible NtMEK2DD system upstream of MPK3/MPK6 activation

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MPK3/MPK6 pathway controlling GAD1; GAD4; indirectly GAD2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required upstream for full induction of GAD1 and GAD4 after Pst-avrRpt2 infection
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Upstream kinase activation of MPK3/MPK6 cascade
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with MKK5 in MKK4/MKK5-MPK3/MPK6 cascade
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen-induced GAD1/GAD4 expression as an upstream component of the MAPK cascade regulating GABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream activator of the MAPK module controlling inducible GABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mkk4 mkk5 double mutant analysis; RT-qPCR after Pst-avrRpt2 inoculation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upstream intervention point for defense metabolism, but likely too pleiotropic for direct engineering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad signaling regulator with extensive immune and growth-related pleiotropy likely
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GABA levels were not reduced in mkk4 mkk5 after Pst-avrRpt2 despite reduced GAD1/GAD4 induction, likely because GAD2 suppression was blocked and/or additional pathways contribute
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MKK4 is part of the upstream kinase module required for pathogen-induced GAD1/GAD4 expression. 2. Loss of MKK4/MKK5 function suppresses GAD1/GAD4 transcription after Pst-avrRpt2. 3. The pathway regulates inducible GABA biosynthesis mainly at the transcriptional level.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MPK3/MPK6 pathway controlling GAD1; GAD4; indirectly GAD2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required upstream for full induction of GAD1 and GAD4 after Pst-avrRpt2 infection
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Upstream kinase activation of MPK3/MPK6 cascade
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with MKK4 in MKK4/MKK5-MPK3/MPK6 cascade
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen-induced GAD1/GAD4 expression as an upstream component of the MAPK cascade regulating GABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream activator of the MAPK module controlling inducible GABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mkk4 mkk5 double mutant analysis; RT-qPCR after Pst-avrRpt2 inoculation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upstream intervention point for defense metabolism, but likely too pleiotropic for direct engineering
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad signaling regulator with extensive immune and growth-related pleiotropy likely
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GABA levels were not reduced in mkk4 mkk5 after Pst-avrRpt2 despite reduced GAD1/GAD4 induction, likely because GAD2 suppression was blocked and/or additional pathways contribute
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MKK5 is part of the upstream kinase module required for pathogen-induced GAD1/GAD4 expression. 2. Loss of MKK4/MKK5 function suppresses GAD1/GAD4 transcription after Pst-avrRpt2. 3. The pathway regulates inducible GABA biosynthesis mainly at the transcriptional level.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional regulation of GABA biosynthesis genes during immunity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes induction of GAD1 and GAD4; contributes to suppression of GAD2 in response to Pst-avrRpt2
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation/reprogramming downstream of MKK4/MKK5-MPK3/MPK6 signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts as downstream substrate of MPK3/MPK6; no additional complex demonstrated here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full pathogen-induced GAD1/GAD4 expression and thereby contributes to inducible GABA biosynthesis during immunity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key downstream transcriptional regulator connecting MAPK immune signaling to GABA biosynthetic gene induction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: wrky33 mutant analysis; RT-qPCR after Pst-avrRpt2 inoculation; inference supported by cited ChIP-seq showing GAD1 as direct target under flg22
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pst-avrRpt2 inoculation; cited flg22 context for direct GAD1 targeting
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible regulatory lever for defense-metabolite induction, but broad defense functions may cause pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad defense TF likely affects multiple immune and metabolic outputs beyond GABA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper cites prior ChIP-seq evidence that GAD1 is a direct WRKY33 target in flg22 response; in this study wrky33 mutation compromises GAD1/GAD4 induction after Pst-avrRpt2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRKY33 acts downstream of the MKK4/MKK5-MPK3/MPK6 cascade to regulate GAD1 and GAD4 expression. 2. wrky33 mutation suppresses GAD1/GAD4 induction during avrRpt2-triggered immunity. 3. WRKY33 links immune signaling to transcriptional activation of GABA biosynthesis genes.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Cited ChIP-seq evidence for direct GAD1 targeting under flg22 (accession NA)
